preFix.title: Pumpkin bread
preFix.subtitle: An American autumn quick bread, warmly spiced
preFix.excerpt: Pumpkin bread is a quick loaf made with pumpkin puree, warming spice, and a straightforward batter. No yeast, no kneading. The pumpkin keeps it moist for days. It works as a breakfast bread, a teatime slice, or a pudding with a little whipped cream.
preFix.sourceNotes: Pumpkin bread is an American quick bread tradition, rooted in the autumn harvest season of the northeastern United States. The basic formula of pumpkin puree, egg, oil, and spiced flour appears in early twentieth-century American home-baking guides and is documented extensively in USDA extension service materials on vegetable-based baking. The spice blend here (cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg) follows the traditional American autumn spice profile.

content.text: Pumpkin bread is a batter loaf rather than a dough, which means it comes together without any kneading. The pumpkin puree provides the bulk of the moisture, so the batter looks quite wet compared with a bread dough: that is correct. The loaf bakes slowly at a moderate temperature, giving a deep-coloured, moist crumb. A skewer through the centre should come out clean at around the 55-minute mark.

Heat the oven to 170 degrees C fan. Grease a 900 g (2 lb) loaf tin and line the base and long sides with a strip of baking paper. In a large bowl, whisk together 250 g plain flour, 1 tsp bicarbonate of soda, half a teaspoon each of baking powder and salt, 1.5 tsp cinnamon, half a teaspoon of ginger, and a quarter teaspoon of nutmeg. In a separate bowl, beat 200 g light brown sugar with 2 large eggs until combined. Add 300 g pumpkin puree, 120 ml vegetable oil, and 1 tsp vanilla extract and mix to combine. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry and fold together with a silicone spatula until just combined. A few remaining streaks of flour are fine; do not overmix or the crumb will be tough.

Scrape the batter into the prepared tin. Bake for 55 to 65 minutes. Start checking at 55 minutes: the loaf is done when a skewer inserted through the centre comes out clean, the top is a deep mahogany brown, and the sides have pulled slightly from the tin. If the top is browning faster than the inside is cooking, lay a piece of foil loosely over the top for the final 15 minutes. Leave in the tin for 10 minutes, then turn out onto a rack and cool for at least 15 minutes before slicing.

items.problem: Bread is gummy in the middle
items.solution: Under-baked. The pumpkin adds considerable moisture, and the loaf needs a full 55 to 65 minutes to set. Cover the top with foil to prevent over-browning and continue baking until the skewer is clean.
The batter was over-mixed. Fold wet into dry until just combined; a few flour streaks remaining is correct. Overmixing develops the gluten and makes the crumb tight.
